questions.prompt: Which best describes a major economic motive for European imperialism in the late 19th century?
choices: The desire to spread Buddhist teachings
The need for raw materials and new markets for manufactured goods
The wish to reduce European population pressure through emigration only
The goal of forming a European federation
questions.correctText: The need for raw materials and new markets for manufactured goods

questions.prompt: Rudyard Kipling's phrase 'the white man's burden' was used most often to justify imperialism by appealing to which idea?
choices: The duty of 'civilized' Europeans to uplift non-European peoples
The economic equality of all races
The need to return colonies to indigenous rule
The unity of Christian and Muslim civilizations
questions.correctText: The duty of 'civilized' Europeans to uplift non-European peoples

questions.prompt: Social Darwinism was most often used in the late 1800s to justify
choices: the abolition of slavery
European domination over Africans and Asians
the spread of democratic government
religious tolerance among colonized peoples
questions.correctText: European domination over Africans and Asians

questions.prompt: Which best describes a 'sphere of influence'?
choices: A colony directly governed by European officials
An area where a foreign power claims exclusive trading and investment rights
An independent nation allied with the United States
A territory ruled by indigenous leaders without outside contact
questions.correctText: An area where a foreign power claims exclusive trading and investment rights

questions.prompt: Indirect rule, as practiced by the British in parts of Africa, generally meant that
choices: European officials ruled every village directly
local traditional leaders governed under European supervision
the colony elected its own parliament
colonies were administered by missionaries alone
questions.correctText: local traditional leaders governed under European supervision

questions.prompt: A major purpose of the Berlin Conference of 1884–1885 was to
choices: end the African slave trade once and for all
set rules for European powers dividing Africa into colonies
grant independence to African kingdoms
create a free-trade zone between Africa and Asia
questions.correctText: set rules for European powers dividing Africa into colonies

questions.prompt: One important result of the Berlin Conference was that
choices: African leaders gained equal representation in European parliaments
European borders were drawn through Africa without regard to ethnic groups
the slave trade became legal again across the Atlantic
Africa was unified into a single empire
questions.correctText: European borders were drawn through Africa without regard to ethnic groups

questions.prompt: King Leopold II of Belgium is most associated with which of the following?
choices: The peaceful decolonization of the Congo
Brutal exploitation of the Congo for rubber and ivory
Founding the Pan-African Congress
Liberating enslaved people in the Caribbean
questions.correctText: Brutal exploitation of the Congo for rubber and ivory

questions.prompt: Which generalization is best supported by the events in the Belgian Congo under Leopold II?
choices: Imperialism always brought clear benefits to colonized peoples
Profit motives could lead to severe human rights abuses in colonies
European public opinion eagerly supported all colonial atrocities
African resistance always succeeded in expelling Europeans
questions.correctText: Profit motives could lead to severe human rights abuses in colonies

questions.prompt: The Boer War (1899–1902) in southern Africa was primarily a conflict between
choices: French settlers and African kingdoms
British forces and Dutch-descended Afrikaners (Boers)
Portuguese traders and Zulu warriors
American missionaries and Belgian colonists
questions.correctText: British forces and Dutch-descended Afrikaners (Boers)

questions.prompt: Shaka, the early 19th-century ruler of the Zulu, is best known for
choices: forming a powerful military kingdom in southern Africa
negotiating a treaty with King Leopold II
leading the Indian National Congress
introducing the Meiji reforms
questions.correctText: forming a powerful military kingdom in southern Africa

questions.prompt: The Battle of Adwa in 1896 is most significant because it showed that
choices: European armies were invincible in Africa
Ethiopia could defeat a European power (Italy) and remain independent
African states preferred European rule to independence
the Berlin Conference had been peacefully accepted
questions.correctText: Ethiopia could defeat a European power (Italy) and remain independent

questions.prompt: The opening of the Suez Canal in 1869 was important because it
choices: shortened the sea route between Europe and Asia
ended the Ottoman Empire's involvement in Egypt
gave the United States control of the Mediterranean
isolated Africa from European trade
questions.correctText: shortened the sea route between Europe and Asia

questions.prompt: The British East India Company is best described as
choices: a religious order founded to spread Hinduism
a trading company that gradually came to govern much of India
an Indian nationalist political party
a Mughal military force
questions.correctText: a trading company that gradually came to govern much of India

questions.prompt: The Sepoy Mutiny of 1857 was a direct response to
choices: British policies and religious insensitivities offending Indian soldiers
the establishment of the Indian National Congress
Gandhi's call for civil disobedience
the partition of Bengal in 1905
questions.correctText: British policies and religious insensitivities offending Indian soldiers

questions.prompt: A direct result of the Sepoy Mutiny was that
choices: India gained immediate independence
the British Crown took direct control of India from the East India Company
France replaced Britain as the colonial power in India
Indian princes were given seats in Parliament
questions.correctText: the British Crown took direct control of India from the East India Company

questions.prompt: India was often called the 'jewel in the crown' of the British Empire because it
choices: was Britain's only colony in Asia
supplied valuable raw materials and a vast market for British goods
was the only colony to receive full self-rule
had no economic value but great symbolic value
questions.correctText: supplied valuable raw materials and a vast market for British goods

questions.prompt: An important economic effect of British rule in India was that
choices: India became a leading exporter of finished textiles to Britain
traditional Indian cottage industries declined as Indians bought British goods
Indian banks dominated the British economy
British factories were closed to protect Indian artisans
questions.correctText: traditional Indian cottage industries declined as Indians bought British goods
questions.topic: Impact of British Rule on India

questions.prompt: The Indian National Congress, founded in 1885, was originally established to
choices: overthrow British rule through armed revolution
give educated Indians a voice in British colonial policy
promote Hindu supremacy over Muslims
support the partition of Bengal
questions.correctText: give educated Indians a voice in British colonial policy

questions.prompt: The British partition of Bengal in 1905 was opposed by many Indians because they believed it was designed to
choices: unite Hindus and Muslims politically
weaken the growing Indian nationalist movement
give Bengal full independence
promote Indian-owned industries
questions.correctText: weaken the growing Indian nationalist movement

questions.prompt: The Opium Wars between Britain and China resulted in
choices: a Chinese victory and the expulsion of all foreigners
unequal treaties that opened Chinese ports and ceded Hong Kong to Britain
the immediate fall of the Qing Dynasty
the closing of all Chinese ports to opium
questions.correctText: unequal treaties that opened Chinese ports and ceded Hong Kong to Britain

questions.prompt: The term 'unequal treaties' in 19th-century China refers to agreements that
choices: gave China equal status with European powers
granted special privileges and extraterritoriality to foreigners in China
ended European trade in Asia
established Chinese colonies in Europe
questions.correctText: granted special privileges and extraterritoriality to foreigners in China

questions.prompt: The Taiping Rebellion of the mid-1800s weakened China primarily by
choices: modernizing the Chinese military
causing massive loss of life and disrupting Qing authority
forcing the British out of Hong Kong
uniting Chinese peasants behind the emperor
questions.correctText: causing massive loss of life and disrupting Qing authority

questions.prompt: By the late 1800s, foreign 'spheres of influence' in China meant that
choices: different European powers and Japan controlled trade in different regions of China
China was divided into formal European colonies
China had successfully resisted all foreign influence
the United States ruled all coastal cities
questions.correctText: different European powers and Japan controlled trade in different regions of China

questions.prompt: The United States's Open Door Policy in China was intended primarily to
choices: guarantee equal trading rights for all foreign powers in China
give the United States exclusive control of China
support the Boxers' attack on foreigners
return Hong Kong to Chinese rule
questions.correctText: guarantee equal trading rights for all foreign powers in China

questions.prompt: The Boxer Rebellion of 1900 was a Chinese uprising against
choices: the Qing Dynasty alone
growing foreign influence and Christian missionaries in China
the Treaty of Versailles
Japanese rule over Korea
questions.correctText: growing foreign influence and Christian missionaries in China

questions.prompt: Sun Yat-sen is best known for
choices: leading the 1911 Revolution that ended the Qing Dynasty
founding the Indian National Congress
negotiating the Treaty of Versailles
modernizing Japan during the Meiji era
questions.correctText: leading the 1911 Revolution that ended the Qing Dynasty

questions.prompt: The 1911 Revolution in China is most directly associated with the
choices: beginning of the Qing Dynasty
establishment of the Republic of China
founding of the Communist Party of China
Boxer Rebellion
questions.correctText: establishment of the Republic of China

questions.prompt: French Indochina in the late 19th century included present-day
choices: Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia
Indonesia and Malaysia
the Philippines and Taiwan
India and Pakistan
questions.correctText: Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia

questions.prompt: The Dutch East Indies became valuable to the Netherlands largely because of its
choices: wheat fields and coal mines
spices, rubber, and other tropical products
shipbuilding industry
uranium deposits
questions.correctText: spices, rubber, and other tropical products

questions.prompt: The United States acquired the Philippines in 1898 as a result of the
choices: Mexican–American War
Spanish–American War
Boxer Rebellion
Open Door Policy
questions.correctText: Spanish–American War

questions.prompt: Japan's Meiji Restoration of 1868 is best described as a
choices: return to feudal rule by powerful samurai
rapid program of modernization while restoring imperial authority
movement to expel all Buddhists from Japan
Western invasion that ended Japanese independence
questions.correctText: rapid program of modernization while restoring imperial authority

questions.prompt: An important effect of the Meiji Restoration was that Japan
choices: rejected all Western technology and ideas
became a modern industrial and military power
remained a closed agricultural society
became a European colony
questions.correctText: became a modern industrial and military power

questions.prompt: Japan's defeat of China in 1895 and Russia in 1905 demonstrated that
choices: Japan was militarily weak compared to its neighbors
an Asian nation could compete with European powers
Western nations no longer engaged in imperialism
China and Russia were natural allies
questions.correctText: an Asian nation could compete with European powers

questions.prompt: Japanese leaders during the Meiji era believed that Japan should
choices: adopt Western technology while preserving Japanese culture and identity
abandon all Japanese traditions for Western customs
isolate itself from the rest of the world
join a Pan-Asian alliance led by China
questions.correctText: adopt Western technology while preserving Japanese culture and identity

questions.prompt: The Monroe Doctrine of 1823 warned European powers that
choices: the Americas were no longer open to further European colonization
the United States would not interfere in any region
Latin America would join the British Empire
Spain could reclaim its former colonies
questions.correctText: the Americas were no longer open to further European colonization

questions.prompt: The Roosevelt Corollary to the Monroe Doctrine asserted that the United States had the right to
choices: intervene in Latin American nations to maintain stability
purchase Caribbean colonies from European powers
join the Triple Alliance
give all Latin American nations independence
questions.correctText: intervene in Latin American nations to maintain stability

questions.prompt: Countries in Central America were often called 'banana republics' because they
choices: were ruled by indigenous monarchs
had economies dominated by American fruit companies
exported large amounts of manufactured goods
had achieved full economic independence
questions.correctText: had economies dominated by American fruit companies

questions.prompt: The construction of the Panama Canal, completed in 1914, was important to the United States because it
choices: allowed faster naval and commercial movement between the Atlantic and Pacific
ended American involvement in Latin America
gave Panama control over global shipping
connected the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea
questions.correctText: allowed faster naval and commercial movement between the Atlantic and Pacific

questions.prompt: Which best describes the Ghadar movement of the early 1900s?
choices: A British missionary society in India
A revolutionary movement of overseas Indians seeking to overthrow British rule
A French campaign to colonize Indochina
A Chinese peasant rebellion against Japan
questions.correctText: A revolutionary movement of overseas Indians seeking to overthrow British rule

questions.prompt: The mfecane in early 19th-century southern Africa refers to a period of
choices: peaceful European settlement of the Cape
widespread warfare and migration associated with the rise of the Zulu
Ottoman expansion into Africa
Indian indentured labor
questions.correctText: widespread warfare and migration associated with the rise of the Zulu

questions.prompt: The Young Turks Revolution of 1908 in the Ottoman Empire sought to
choices: restore the absolute power of the sultan
establish a constitutional government and modernize the empire
create an independent Arab state
abolish Islam as the state religion
questions.correctText: establish a constitutional government and modernize the empire

questions.prompt: The Armenian Genocide that began in 1915 was carried out by the
choices: Russian Empire against Polish minorities
Ottoman government against the Armenian population
British Empire in India
Habsburgs against Slavic peoples
questions.correctText: Ottoman government against the Armenian population

questions.prompt: The acronym M.A.I.N. used to describe the long-term causes of World War I stands for
choices: Militarism, Alliances, Imperialism, Nationalism
Markets, Armies, Industry, Navies
Monarchy, Authority, Inequality, Neutrality
Migration, Anarchy, Industry, Nationalism
questions.correctText: Militarism, Alliances, Imperialism, Nationalism

questions.prompt: Which best defines militarism as a cause of World War I?
choices: A commitment to peaceful diplomacy among nations
Glorification of armed forces and a buildup of military power
An economic policy of free trade
Opposition to any standing army
questions.correctText: Glorification of armed forces and a buildup of military power

questions.prompt: Before World War I, the Triple Alliance was made up of
choices: Britain, France, and Russia
Germany, Austria-Hungary, and Italy
the United States, Japan, and China
Russia, Serbia, and the Ottoman Empire
questions.correctText: Germany, Austria-Hungary, and Italy

questions.prompt: Before World War I, the Triple Entente was made up of
choices: Germany, Austria-Hungary, and the Ottoman Empire
Britain, France, and Russia
Italy, Spain, and Portugal
Japan, China, and the United States
questions.correctText: Britain, France, and Russia

questions.prompt: Nationalism contributed to World War I most directly by
choices: encouraging multi-ethnic empires to stay united
inspiring ethnic groups in the Balkans to seek independence
ending all rivalries between European powers
discouraging military buildup
questions.correctText: inspiring ethnic groups in the Balkans to seek independence

questions.prompt: The Balkans were referred to as the 'powder keg of Europe' because the region was characterized by
choices: religious unity and political stability
competing nationalisms and great-power rivalries
lack of ethnic diversity
isolation from European affairs
questions.correctText: competing nationalisms and great-power rivalries

questions.prompt: A direct cause of World War I was the
choices: sinking of the Lusitania
assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo
Bolshevik Revolution
signing of the Treaty of Versailles
questions.correctText: assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo

questions.prompt: Archduke Franz Ferdinand was heir to the throne of which empire?
choices: Russian Empire
Austro-Hungarian Empire
German Empire
Ottoman Empire
questions.correctText: Austro-Hungarian Empire

questions.prompt: Which generalization is best supported by the events leading to World War I?
choices: A single assassination has never had political consequences
Entangling alliances can turn a local conflict into a wider war
Empires that ignore nationalism remain stable
Disarmament always prevents major wars
questions.correctText: Entangling alliances can turn a local conflict into a wider war

questions.prompt: The Schlieffen Plan was Germany's strategy to
choices: defeat France quickly through Belgium and then turn east to fight Russia
negotiate a separate peace with Russia
invade Britain through the English Channel
avoid war by isolating Austria-Hungary
questions.correctText: defeat France quickly through Belgium and then turn east to fight Russia

questions.prompt: Trench warfare on the Western Front during World War I generally resulted in
choices: rapid, sweeping victories for both sides
long periods of stalemate and massive casualties
fast cavalry battles like those of the Napoleonic era
the immediate use of nuclear weapons
questions.correctText: long periods of stalemate and massive casualties

questions.prompt: Which new weapon technology most dramatically increased the casualty rates on the Western Front?
choices: The longbow
The machine gun and poison gas
The catapult
Naval mines used in the Mediterranean only
questions.correctText: The machine gun and poison gas

questions.prompt: Submarine warfare in World War I is most associated with
choices: British convoys in the Mediterranean only
German U-boats targeting Allied shipping
Russian fleets in the Baltic Sea
American naval blockades of Japan
questions.correctText: German U-boats targeting Allied shipping

questions.prompt: The sinking of the Lusitania in 1915 contributed to
choices: the immediate Russian withdrawal from the war
growing American hostility toward Germany
the formation of the Triple Alliance
the abdication of the Ottoman sultan
questions.correctText: growing American hostility toward Germany

questions.prompt: The United States entered World War I in 1917 largely in response to
choices: the Russian Revolution
unrestricted German submarine warfare and the Zimmermann Telegram
the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand
the rise of fascism in Italy
questions.correctText: unrestricted German submarine warfare and the Zimmermann Telegram

questions.prompt: Russia withdrew from World War I in 1918 mainly because
choices: it had decisively defeated Germany
revolution at home and military exhaustion forced peace with Germany
it had captured Berlin
it switched to the Central Powers
questions.correctText: revolution at home and military exhaustion forced peace with Germany

questions.prompt: The Treaty of Brest-Litovsk (1918) was significant because it
choices: ended World War I for all combatants
took Russia out of World War I and gave large territories to Germany
established the League of Nations
joined Russia to the Triple Entente
questions.correctText: took Russia out of World War I and gave large territories to Germany

questions.prompt: World War I has been called a 'total war' because
choices: it involved only professional soldiers
entire societies and economies were mobilized for the war effort
it took place entirely in Europe
it lasted less than one year
questions.correctText: entire societies and economies were mobilized for the war effort

questions.prompt: During World War I, governments used propaganda primarily to
choices: maintain public support and encourage enlistment and sacrifice
negotiate peace with the enemy
promote international socialism
weaken their own war effort
questions.correctText: maintain public support and encourage enlistment and sacrifice

questions.prompt: A major social change in many warring nations during World War I was that
choices: women took on factory and other jobs traditionally held by men
women were banned from the workforce
child labor was abolished worldwide
labor unions were eliminated
questions.correctText: women took on factory and other jobs traditionally held by men

questions.prompt: President Woodrow Wilson's Fourteen Points are best described as
choices: a plan to punish Germany severely
a set of principles for a just postwar peace, including self-determination
a treaty between Russia and Germany
a declaration of war by the United States
questions.correctText: a set of principles for a just postwar peace, including self-determination

questions.prompt: The principle of self-determination, as expressed by Wilson, meant that
choices: European powers should keep all colonies indefinitely
national groups should have the right to choose their own government
only Germany would lose territory
monarchies should remain in place across Europe
questions.correctText: national groups should have the right to choose their own government

questions.prompt: The armistice ending World War I was signed on
choices: November 11, 1918
December 7, 1941
August 14, 1945
questions.correctText: November 11, 1918

questions.prompt: The Treaty of Versailles in 1919 placed responsibility for World War I primarily on
choices: Russia and Serbia
Germany, through the war-guilt clause
France and Britain
Austria-Hungary alone
questions.correctText: Germany, through the war-guilt clause

questions.prompt: Reparations required by the Treaty of Versailles meant that Germany had to
choices: pay large sums for damages caused by the war
give up its monarchy and become a colony
send troops to fight in Russia
accept American occupation of Berlin
questions.correctText: pay large sums for damages caused by the war

questions.prompt: Under the Treaty of Versailles, the German military was
choices: greatly expanded to keep the peace
severely limited in size and weapons
merged with the French army
given control of new colonies
questions.correctText: severely limited in size and weapons

questions.prompt: The 'mandates' established after World War I were
choices: independent nations created from former Allied colonies
former Ottoman and German territories administered by Allied powers under the League
trade agreements between Germany and Russia
American protectorates in Asia
questions.correctText: former Ottoman and German territories administered by Allied powers under the League

questions.prompt: The League of Nations was established mainly to
choices: punish Germany economically forever
settle international disputes peacefully and prevent another world war
spread communism around the world
promote European colonization of Africa
questions.correctText: settle international disputes peacefully and prevent another world war

questions.prompt: A major weakness of the League of Nations was that
choices: the United States did not join
it controlled large armies
only African nations were members
it had no permanent headquarters
questions.correctText: the United States did not join

questions.prompt: Which group of empires collapsed as a direct result of World War I?
choices: French, Portuguese, Dutch, and Spanish
Ottoman, German, Austro-Hungarian, and Russian
British, Belgian, Italian, and Japanese
Persian, Mughal, Aztec, and Inca
questions.correctText: Ottoman, German, Austro-Hungarian, and Russian

questions.prompt: Which best describes a major political effect of World War I in central Europe?
choices: The creation of new nation-states such as Poland and Czechoslovakia
The unification of Germany and Austria into one empire
The return of feudalism
The expansion of the Ottoman Empire
questions.correctText: The creation of new nation-states such as Poland and Czechoslovakia

questions.prompt: Bloody Sunday in St. Petersburg in 1905 is best described as
choices: a peaceful workers' march that was fired upon by the tsar's troops
a successful Bolshevik takeover of the government
the assassination of Nicholas II
the start of World War I
questions.correctText: a peaceful workers' march that was fired upon by the tsar's troops

questions.prompt: The 1905 Revolution in Russia forced Tsar Nicholas II to
choices: abdicate immediately
issue the October Manifesto and create the Duma
abolish the monarchy permanently
join the Bolshevik Party
questions.correctText: issue the October Manifesto and create the Duma

questions.prompt: The February Revolution of 1917 in Russia resulted in
choices: the abdication of Tsar Nicholas II and the formation of a Provisional Government
the immediate establishment of communism
Russia's victory over Germany
the restoration of serfdom
questions.correctText: the abdication of Tsar Nicholas II and the formation of a Provisional Government

questions.prompt: One major weakness of the Russian Provisional Government in 1917 was its decision to
choices: continue Russia's involvement in World War I
redistribute all land to peasants immediately
outlaw the Orthodox Church
abolish the Duma
questions.correctText: continue Russia's involvement in World War I

questions.prompt: The Bolshevik slogan 'Peace, Land, and Bread' appealed to Russians because it promised to
choices: expand the Russian Empire into Africa
end the war, redistribute land, and address food shortages
restore the tsar to power
industrialize agriculture under the Duma
questions.correctText: end the war, redistribute land, and address food shortages

questions.prompt: Vladimir Lenin and the Bolsheviks came to power in Russia through the
choices: February Revolution of 1917
October Revolution of 1917
Boxer Rebellion
Decembrist Revolt
questions.correctText: October Revolution of 1917

questions.prompt: The Russian Civil War (1918–1921) was primarily fought between
choices: the Bolshevik 'Reds' and anti-Bolshevik 'Whites'
Russia and Germany
the Duma and the tsar
Mensheviks and Cossacks only, with no foreign involvement
questions.correctText: the Bolshevik 'Reds' and anti-Bolshevik 'Whites'

questions.prompt: Lenin's New Economic Policy (NEP) in the early 1920s
choices: allowed limited private enterprise to revive the economy
fully collectivized all Russian farms
ended Communist Party control of Russia
reopened Russia to foreign colonization
questions.correctText: allowed limited private enterprise to revive the economy

questions.prompt: Joseph Stalin came to power in the Soviet Union
choices: before the October Revolution
after Lenin's death in 1924
during the Russo-Japanese War
as a result of the Treaty of Versailles
questions.correctText: after Lenin's death in 1924

questions.prompt: Stalin's Five-Year Plans were designed primarily to
choices: restore private property in Russia
rapidly industrialize the Soviet Union under state control
create a multi-party democracy
expand religious freedom
questions.correctText: rapidly industrialize the Soviet Union under state control

questions.prompt: Collectivization of agriculture under Stalin meant that
choices: peasants combined their small farms into large state-controlled farms
private farming was encouraged and protected by law
peasants received their own private land for the first time
agriculture was abandoned in favor of industry only
questions.correctText: peasants combined their small farms into large state-controlled farms

questions.prompt: The Ukrainian famine of 1932–1933, known as the Holodomor, was largely a result of
choices: a natural drought with no political cause
Stalin's collectivization and grain-requisition policies
the withdrawal of foreign aid
the policies of the Provisional Government
questions.correctText: Stalin's collectivization and grain-requisition policies

questions.prompt: Stalin's Great Purges of the 1930s involved
choices: mass arrests, executions, and show trials of suspected opponents
the peaceful retirement of Communist Party leaders
negotiations with Trotsky to share power
free elections across the Soviet Union
questions.correctText: mass arrests, executions, and show trials of suspected opponents

questions.prompt: Which best describes totalitarianism as practiced under Stalin?
choices: A government with strict limits on its power and protected civil rights
A government that seeks total control over public and private life
A democracy with multiple competing political parties
A government that protects independent labor unions
questions.correctText: A government that seeks total control over public and private life

questions.prompt: A common feature of both Stalin's Soviet Union and other totalitarian states is the use of
choices: free press and open elections
secret police and state-controlled media
independent religious courts
decentralized federal government
questions.correctText: secret police and state-controlled media

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes a long-term cause of World War I?
choices: The competition for colonies and markets among European powers
The signing of the Treaty of Versailles
The Bolshevik Revolution
The opening of the Suez Canal in 1869
questions.correctText: The competition for colonies and markets among European powers

questions.prompt: Which generalization is best supported by Japan's actions from 1868 to 1905?
choices: Modernization can transform a traditional society into a major power
Isolation is the surest path to industrial development
Western powers always block Asian modernization
Cultural traditions prevent industrialization
questions.correctText: Modernization can transform a traditional society into a major power

questions.prompt: An important political effect of imperialism in Africa was that
choices: traditional ethnic boundaries were strengthened
new political boundaries often ignored cultural divisions
Africans gained equal voting rights in European parliaments
African kingdoms expanded into Europe
questions.correctText: new political boundaries often ignored cultural divisions

questions.prompt: Which document or speech is most associated with the idea of national self-determination after World War I?
choices: The Communist Manifesto
Wilson's Fourteen Points
The Treaty of Brest-Litovsk
The Berlin Conference's Final Act
questions.correctText: Wilson's Fourteen Points

questions.prompt: The breakup of the Ottoman Empire after World War I led most directly to
choices: the formation of the modern Middle East under European mandates
the unification of all Arab lands under one ruler
the restoration of the Byzantine Empire
the immediate independence of Palestine and Iraq as nation-states
questions.correctText: the formation of the modern Middle East under European mandates

questions.prompt: Which best describes a major effect of World War I on the map of Europe?
choices: The Austro-Hungarian Empire broke into several independent nation-states
European borders remained almost exactly as they had been in 1914
Germany expanded to include all of central Europe
Russia annexed France and Britain
questions.correctText: The Austro-Hungarian Empire broke into several independent nation-states

questions.prompt: A primary source from a Vietnamese village in French Indochina would most likely describe
choices: French taxation, conscription, and disruption of local economies
Vietnamese conquest of the French navy
Vietnamese settlers ruling parts of Paris
the absence of any French presence
questions.correctText: French taxation, conscription, and disruption of local economies

questions.prompt: Which factor most directly enabled European powers to dominate African and Asian peoples in the 19th century?
choices: A larger population than Asia and Africa combined
Industrial technology, including steamships, railroads, and modern weapons
Greater natural resources within Europe itself
More fertile farmland than the rest of the world
questions.correctText: Industrial technology, including steamships, railroads, and modern weapons

questions.prompt: Which is the best example of a 'protectorate' in the age of imperialism?
choices: A region directly governed by a European parliament
A country whose own ruler remained in place but was controlled by a foreign power
An independent ally of a European empire with no foreign control
A self-governing colony with full voting rights
questions.correctText: A country whose own ruler remained in place but was controlled by a foreign power

questions.prompt: Which conclusion is best supported by comparing the Russian Revolution of 1917 with the fall of the Qing Dynasty in 1911?
choices: Long-standing social and economic problems can contribute to the collapse of empires
Empires are usually overthrown by foreign invasion alone
Revolutions never succeed without democratic constitutions
Reform movements always preserve traditional monarchies
questions.correctText: Long-standing social and economic problems can contribute to the collapse of empires

questions.prompt: Which statement best summarizes a connection between World War I and the Russian Revolution?
choices: Russia's poor performance in the war intensified discontent and contributed to revolution
World War I had no effect on the Russian Revolution
Russia won the war and then experienced a peaceful revolution
Lenin's Bolsheviks led Russia to victory in the war
questions.correctText: Russia's poor performance in the war intensified discontent and contributed to revolution
